Just now

--

If you’ve worked with search, recommendation systems, or anything touching large language models, you’ve probably used the words “tokenization” and “embeddings” without stopping to unpack what’s actually happening underneath.
Let’s take the example of a job seeker with skills in —
“Backend Engineer with Kafka Experience”
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Photo by Resume Genius on UnsplashThe Problem —
To Machines if they need to understand text we need to do math on text
Neural networks are, at their core, machines that do math on numbers. They don’t understand the string “Kafka” any more than they understand a photograph of a cat. Before any model can process language, that language has to be converted into numbers and that conversion happens in two distinct stages:
first tokenization then embedding.
Let’s understand tokenisation in detail.
1) Tokenization
Tokenization is the process of breaking raw text into smaller units, called tokens, that a model can process. The naive approach would be splitting on whitespace and treating every word as one unit. But this breaks down fast.
